caveats: Coding and facility/professional bundle boundaries must be manually validated.
policy_insight: Identifies whether schedules structurally privilege procedures over diagnostic reasoning, chronic care, counselling and coordination.
primary_output: Jurisdiction-level index and service-pair basket table.
purpose: Compare reimbursement relativities for consultative/cognitive work against procedural/interventional work.
title: Cognitive versus procedural reward index

caveats: CPT/MBS/test-directory mapping requires clinical review and LOINC/HPO support.
policy_insight: Shows whether public coverage produces actual uptake and whether restrictions create bottlenecks.
primary_output: Genomic test graph, coverage chronology and utilisation panel.
purpose: Map genomic test eligibility, price/payment, coverage criteria and utilisation trajectories.
title: Genomics coverage, price and diffusion atlas

caveats: Net prices are often unavailable; output should be labelled as transparency, not exact net price.
policy_insight: Highlights hidden rebates, confidential deeds, out-of-pocket gaps and negotiated plan/hospital prices.
purpose: Score each schedule on how far public prices are from likely effective payer cost.
title: Published versus effective price opacity score

caveats: Can become descriptive unless linked to case studies.
policy_insight: Clarifies institutional design choices: national vs local discretion, explicit HTA, evidence thresholds and appeals.
purpose: Compare how systems separate evidence assessment, coverage, price setting, restrictions and utilisation management.
title: Coverage decision architecture comparison

caveats: Needs careful assumptions and sometimes non-public plan/provider pricing.
policy_insight: Shows whether public reimbursement actually protects patients from out-of-pocket exposure.
primary_output: Cost exposure profiles and simulated patient vignettes.
purpose: Compare copayment, coinsurance, balance billing, bulk billing and safety-net rules for matched service baskets.
title: Patient cost exposure and gap risk

caveats: US and Canada are more subnational; Australia variation is more provider supply than schedule rules.
policy_insight: Distinguishes responsiveness from inequity: local coverage can speed innovation or create access variation.
purpose: Measure geographic coverage/payment variation within systems.
title: Local discretion and postcode-lottery index

caveats: DRG systems are not one-to-one; often needs procedure-level vignettes.
policy_insight: Shows whether apparent cross-country price differences are driven by bundling rather than generosity.
primary_output: Bundle decomposition schema and exemplar procedures.
purpose: Compare facility, professional, device, drug and pathology components across hospital-related interventions.
title: Hospital-professional unbundling map

caveats: Regulatory approval dates may require additional data sources.
policy_insight: Reveals whether systems trade speed for certainty, price discipline or evidentiary rigor.
purpose: Measure listing lag and coverage lag for high-cost drugs, genomic tests and devices.
title: Time from evidence/approval to reimbursement

caveats: Needs reliable utilisation time series and mapping families.
policy_insight: Helps distinguish productive innovation from additive low-value growth.
purpose: Test whether new reimbursement items substitute for older items or expand total service volume.
title: Substitution and volume-control evaluation

caveats: Restrictions may be embedded in PDFs and clinical forms.
policy_insight: Turns opaque listing text into auditable access constraints.
purpose: Convert reimbursement restrictions into structured criteria and compare strictness.
title: Medicine restriction-text strictness index

caveats: Some ontologies have licence restrictions and cannot be mirrored.
policy_insight: Identifies data infrastructure gaps that limit comparative policy analysis.
purpose: Assess how well each schedule links to standard terminologies like LOINC, ATC, ICD, SNOMED CT, HPO and RxNorm.
title: Ontology and mapping maturity score

caveats: A schedule can be transparent while effective net payment remains opaque.
policy_insight: Creates a policy transparency index across public reimbursement systems.
purpose: Score public accessibility, machine readability, historical availability, versioning and provenance.
title: Schedule transparency benchmark

caveats: Requires current evidence and regulatory source expansion.
policy_insight: Anticipates policy gaps before AI diagnostics diffuse.
purpose: Assess whether schedules can accommodate algorithmic diagnostics, software as a medical device and AI-supported testing.
title: AI diagnostics reimbursement readiness

caveats: Payment loadings may not translate into service availability.
policy_insight: Shows where reimbursement corrects or compounds geographic access inequity.
purpose: Map explicit rural, remote, deprivation or workforce-adjustment mechanisms.
title: Equity weighting and rurality adjustments

caveats: Price changes can reflect coding changes, bundling changes or policy shocks rather than pure revaluation.
policy_insight: Identifies whether schedules actively manage inflation, technology maturity and budget pressure or allow legacy relativities to persist.
primary_output: Versioned price-change panel and revision-timing dashboard.
purpose: Measure how often and how predictably schedules revise prices, weights and relative values.
title: Price revision velocity and indexation discipline

caveats: Local discretion may be informal and operational rather than explicit in published schedules.
policy_insight: Distinguishes responsive local adaptation from postcode-lottery risk and maps where national schedules still hide local access rules.
purpose: Compare whether coverage decisions are centralised nationally or delegated to local payers, contractors, regions or insurers.
title: Local versus national coverage discretion map

caveats: Confidential price agreements and compassionate-access pathways can be invisible.
policy_insight: Shows whether systems rely on HTA flexibility, managed entry, exceptional funding, local discretion or delayed access.
primary_output: Rare-disease pathway typology and case-study matrix.
purpose: Map how high-uncertainty, low-volume technologies enter public reimbursement systems.
title: Rare disease and ultra-orphan reimbursement pathways

caveats: Hospital procurement discounts and private contracts may dominate effective prices.
policy_insight: Reveals where device policy is transparent enough for price comparison versus obscured by procurement and episode bundling.
primary_output: Device visibility index and exemplar device basket.
purpose: Compare whether devices, prostheses and supplies are itemised, bundled or hidden inside facility payments.
title: Device and prosthesis price visibility comparison

caveats: Temporary pandemic policies and payer-specific rules require careful date handling.
policy_insight: Shows whether virtual care is treated as equivalent care, a lower-cost substitute, or a tightly restricted exception.
primary_output: Telehealth rule matrix and fee-parity ratio table.
purpose: Compare telehealth itemisation, parity, modality restrictions and post-pandemic persistence.
title: Telehealth payment architecture and parity rules

caveats: Capitation, quality payments and grants may sit outside item-level fee schedules.
policy_insight: Indicates whether public payment systems encourage episodic visits or longitudinal population management.
primary_output: Primary-care incentive typology and matched service basket.
purpose: Map how schedules pay for enrolment, chronic disease management, preventive care and care planning.
title: Primary-care longitudinal incentive architecture

caveats: Facility payment and professional payment are often not directly additive across systems.
policy_insight: Shows how bundling choices shape incentives for sites of care, device use and diagnostic ordering.
primary_output: Bundling boundary map and cross-setting payment comparison.
purpose: Compare when services remain separately payable versus absorbed into outpatient or inpatient bundles.
title: Hospital outpatient carve-out and bundling comparison

caveats: Some terminologies are clinically essential but cannot be mirrored publicly.
policy_insight: Makes explicit where licensing or terminology access can block otherwise public reimbursement analysis.
primary_output: Analysis-by-ontology dependency matrix and licence-risk register.
purpose: Identify which analyses require LOINC, RxNorm, ATC, ICD, SNOMED CT, HPO or other terminologies.
title: Terminology coverage and ontology-dependency audit

caveats: Listing dates, first-claim dates and source refresh dates may differ materially.
policy_insight: Separates nominal access from realised access and highlights implementation bottlenecks.
purpose: Measure how long it takes for newly reimbursed services or medicines to diffuse after public listing.
title: Coverage-to-utilisation lag after listing

caveats: Scores reflect public accessibility, not necessarily health-system performance.
policy_insight: Ranks where comparative reimbursement research is easiest, most reproducible and most licence-constrained.
purpose: Score each jurisdiction on machine-readability, licensing, historical versions, API access and utilisation linkage.
title: Global public schedule access and reproducibility index

caveats: Rebates and confidential agreements limit interpretation of net prices.
policy_insight: Identifies where public policy accelerates price convergence and where published prices remain opaque.
primary_output: Therapeutic-class price ladder and policy mechanism map.
purpose: Compare how systems group medicines, reward generics/biosimilars and revise prices after competition.
title: Medicine substitution, reference pricing and price ladder analysis
